ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for NIST control mapping: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect NIST control mapping operational-excellence behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for NIST control mapping.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for NIST control mapping critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Security and Compliance Packs NIST control mapping tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: nist-control-mapping_operational-excellence_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for NIST control mapping operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for NIST control mapping; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for NIST control mapping.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for NIST control mapping recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for NIST control mapping.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for NIST control mapping.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing NIST control mapping data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling NIST control mapping tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for NIST control mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Given NIST control mapping data exists, when the operational-excellence tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: NIST control mapping connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect AWS Foundational Security Best Practices resilience behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Security and Compliance Packs AWS Foundational Security Best Practices tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: aws-foundational-security-best-practices_resilience_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing AWS Foundational Security Best Practices data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling AWS Foundational Security Best Practices tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for AWS Foundational Security Best Practices.
acceptance_criteria: Given AWS Foundational Security Best Practices data exists, when the resilience tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: AWS Foundational Security Best Practices connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for control evidence snapshot: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover control evidence snapshot, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the scalability pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for control evidence snapshot.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for control evidence snapshot.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Security and Compliance Packs control evidence snapshot inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: control-evidence-snapshot_scalability_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for control evidence snapshot scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for control evidence snapshot; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for control evidence snapshot.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for control evidence snapshot recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for control evidence snapshot.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for control evidence snapshot.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing control evidence snapshot data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling control evidence snapshot inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for control evidence snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Given control evidence snapshot data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: control evidence snapshot connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for compliance dashboard: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect compliance dashboard symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect scalability.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for compliance dashboard.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for compliance dashboard.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Security and Compliance Packs compliance dashboard health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: compliance-dashboard_scalability_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for compliance dashboard scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for compliance dashboard; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for compliance dashboard.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for compliance dashboard recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for compliance dashboard.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for compliance dashboard.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing compliance dashboard data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling compliance dashboard health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for compliance dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Given compliance dashboard data exists, when the scalability health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: compliance dashboard connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
